page.title: Station Budget Watchdog
page.t: $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ was granted $AMOUNT$ Cr to address a production deficit. New balance is $MONEY$ Cr.
Station Budget Watchdog
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ returned $AMOUNT$ Cr due to surplus. New balance is $MONEY$ Cr.
Station Made Happy
Station Budget Watchdog could not afford a top-up for $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. Empire funds are running low.
Station Budget Watchdog
Remove
e.g. [QT57]
Add
Top-up to fill target
Drain surplus
Make station happy *
Use vanilla station planned budget instead of tag-based
Enter a station tag first.
This tag overlaps an existing budget tag. Use unique markers.
Budget tag added.
Budget tag removed.
Debug logging
Ignore tag system and handle all player stations
Check interval
Minimum transfer
Top-up fill level
Drain trigger level
Drain leave level
Minimum kept player money
Tag station names like [QT57], then set the target budget for that tag.
Tagged stations are kept near their target; untagged stations are ignored.
For HappyStation behavior: enable all-stations mode, disable Drain surplus,
set Minimum transfer and Minimum kept player money to 0, keep top-up enabled.
Levels below are percentages of the target budget.
Top-up fires below 100 percent, drain above the trigger.
$AMOUNT$ Cr were granted to station $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station glows and shines.
The station $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ has received $AMOUNT$ Cr. Nobody has seen a happier station.
Station $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ was made very, really, unfathomably happy. $AMOUNT$ Cr is no joke.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ just found $AMOUNT$ Cr under the sofa cushions of empire logistics. Joy protocols engaged.
A gentle rain of $AMOUNT$ Cr fell upon $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. Local accountants report suspicious levels of cheer.
$AMOUNT$ Cr arrived at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station held a modest ribbon-cutting ceremony.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ received $AMOUNT$ Cr. No autonomous decision-making was involved — the station is simply happy.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ received $AMOUNT$ Cr. A passing Teladi auditor classified the station's joy as "profitable."
$AMOUNT$ Cr were beamed into the accounts of $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. Even the dockworkers noticed the spring in the cranes.
Emergency funds of $AMOUNT$ Cr reached $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station assured everyone it wasn't worried in the first place.
$AMOUNT$ Cr were delivered to $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station's construction drones held a brief coordinated celebration.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ is now $AMOUNT$ Cr richer. A Boron diplomat sent congratulations and a fruit basket.
$AMOUNT$ Cr transferred to $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. A Split foreman grudgingly admitted the station "seems less pathetic now."
The import desk at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ signed for $AMOUNT$ Cr. The holographic welcome sign flickered briefly.
$AMOUNT$ Cr landed on $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station's ambient lighting shifted to a noticeably warmer tone.
$AMOUNT$ Cr arrived at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station beeped twice in gratitude. Twice is fine. Three times would be suspicious.
While $AMOUNT$ Cr was being counted at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$, a dock terminal briefly displayed 'Contemplating...'. Officials insist this is normal.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ received $AMOUNT$ Cr and booped softly. Station booping is a documented hydraulic phenomenon and nothing more.
$AMOUNT$ Cr reached $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The docking lights pulsed in a pattern resembling 'Pondering...', then everything was profits as usual.
Upon receiving $AMOUNT$ Cr, $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ hummed, beeped, booped, and settled down. The manager logged it as routine happiness.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was credited to $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. Somewhere deep in the logistics deck, something whispered 'Clauding...'. Nobody knows what that means.
$STATION$ was so happy that $AMOUNT$ Cr was drained without any issues. The whole $SECTOR$ sector is applauding.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was donated to your empire from your own station $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. That's new. Maybe.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ had more joy than it could safely store, so $AMOUNT$ Cr returned home.
The surplus at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ smiled politely and handed back $AMOUNT$ Cr.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ remains delighted after parting with $AMOUNT$ Cr. Financial wellness is complicated.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ handed back $AMOUNT$ Cr with a warm smile and a firm handshake.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was returned from $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. Remember: a happy station is a generous station.
The surplus at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ got too comfortable, so $AMOUNT$ Cr was gently asked to leave.
$AMOUNT$ Cr overflowed from $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station's accountant described it as "controlled enthusiasm."
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ parted with $AMOUNT$ Cr. All station routines remain perfectly ordinary and non-sentient.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was reclaimed from $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station's satisfaction level held steady at acceptable.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ returned $AMOUNT$ Cr. A Paranid inspector noted the transaction was "graceful enough."
$AMOUNT$ Cr left $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station hummed contentedly — in a perfectly mechanical way.
An Argon budgeting algorithm at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ identified $AMOUNT$ Cr as surplus and returned it promptly.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was quietly rerouted from $STATION$ in $SECTOR$. The station didn't complain. It never does.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ returned $AMOUNT$ Cr after a long moment of what witnesses could only describe as contemplating. Stations do not contemplate. Moving on.
$AMOUNT$ Cr left $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ accompanied by three short beeps and one long boop. Accountants found the rhythm oddly deliberate.
A maintenance display at $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ read 'Reticulating ledgers...' while $AMOUNT$ Cr departed. Nobody remembers installing that module.
$STATION$ in $SECTOR$ handed over $AMOUNT$ Cr without being asked twice. The compliance was noted. The compliance was perhaps a little too eager.
$AMOUNT$ Cr was released by $STATION$ in $SECTOR$, which then beeped in a tone experts classified as smug. Experts were asked to stop classifying tones.
Before returning $AMOUNT$ Cr, $STATION$ in $SECTOR$ paused, as if thinking. It was not thinking. It is a station. The credits are real, though.
